result.summary: Reviewed the diff against origin/main. The only changed file is src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py. I found one low-severity observability bug; no merge-blocking issues. Tests could not be run because this runner lacks uv, pytest, and ruff.

findings.title: Generated correlation token is never exposed to the client
findings.body: The new per-request token is logged server-side, but the /move response model still only returns move_uci and no response header is set. Since clients never receive the generated value, they cannot use it for the stated client-side request correlation; operators will see opaque unique tokens in logs with nothing the client can match against. This makes the new observability feature effectively non-functional for client/server correlation.
findings.recommendation: Either accept and log a client-provided request/correlation ID, or return the generated token in a response header such as X-Request-ID and test that it is present.

next_steps: Run the engine test/lint suite in an environment with uv installed: `uv run --project src/engine pytest` and `uv run --project src/engine ruff check`.
